rag_rules: Place output range guarantees in component type annexes when bounds are concrete or visible
Use assign statements in implementation annexes only for existing output features or auxiliary variables
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation annexes
Use valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for declarations
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers when available for expressions
Avoid direct references to AADL properties in AGREE expressions
Align helper declaration types with expression types
Use concrete bounds or visible relations for range assumptions and guarantees
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts use assume, guarantee, eq, const, and assign statements
Component type annexes contain assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes contain implementation-level bindings like assignments
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation annexes
Do not use invalid AGREE scalar type names like integer or boolean
